channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sweat the small stuff: Data protection in the age of AI
item.description: As concerns about AI security, risk, and compliance continue to escalate, practical solutions remain elusive.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What to do if someone is shading you online
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: US government removes Chinese chip giant AMEC from major blacklist
item.description: Two prominent Chinese companies have been dropped from the Department of Defense’s concerning companies list.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google’s contractors told to evaluate Gemini responses outside their expertise
item.description: Behind the responses from genAI models are testers who evaluate those answers for accuracy, but a report released this week casts doubt on the process.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hisense's Saturn wireless Dolby Atmos speakers could be a huge hit, based on the last Hisense soundbar I reviewed
item.description: Hisense reveals a Dolby Atmos speaker system as a wireless alternative to a soundbar – and it looks set to take on the Sony Bravia Quad.
item.dc:creator: james.davidson@futurenet.com (James Davidson), TechRadar

item.title: Spending on cloud infrastructure could actually fall in 2025
item.description: Cloud spend is predicted to rise considerably in 2025, but what about the areas predictions don’t cover?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Repsol se adhiere al Pacto por la IA de la Unión Europea
item.description: Como ya viene siendo costumbre, la inteligencia artificial (IA) no ha dejado de copar titulares recientemente. La puerta abierta por parte de las autoridades europeas de permitir que se entrenen modelos de IA con datos personales sin el consentimiento explícito del propietario ha levantado ampollas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk floods X with spending bill misinformation
item.description: Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk unleashed a flood of misinformation about Congress’s end-of-year funding deal Wednesday, as he pushed Republicans to torpedo the stopgap bill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Create searchable Bluesky bookmarks with R
item.description: The Bluesky social network is seeping into the mainstream, thanks in part to people unhappy with changes at X (formerly Twitter). “It’s not some untouched paradise, but it’s got a spark of something refreshing,” Jan Beger, global head of AI Advocacy at GE HealthCare, posted on LinkedIn last month.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Getting started with Azure Managed Redis
item.description: Microsoft has made a lot of big bets in its preferred cloud-native infrastructure. You only need to look at .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data on animal movements help Hungarian researchers create a swarm of autonomous drones
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Moving in a dense cloud, like throngs of people walking across a crowded public square, 100 drones maneuver through the night sky in Hungary's capital, the result of over a decade of research and experimentation that scientists believe could change the future of unmanned fli
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Supreme Court will hear arguments over the TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok has once last chance in court to fight its impending ban. The Supreme Court on Wednesday agreed to take up the social media company’s appeal challenging a federal law that would likely ban the app as soon as next month. The nation’s highest court will hear oral arguments on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Flexibility or security? Trick question: It’s a false choice. Here’s what to do instead
item.description: Remember when 'work' was a place? Federal agencies rethink BYOD to balance security and usability. The post Flexibility or security? Trick question: It’s a false choice. Here’s what to do instead first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: That text from the US Postal Service could be a scam. How to avoid ‘smishing’ fraud
item.description: Be careful in the final week before Christmas, because scammers are trying to use text messages to take advantage of you.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: How to work with text colors on Linux
item.description: When you list files on the Linux command line, many of the file names show up in colors, as you’ve probably noticed. The colors used depend on the file types. For example, the names of directories will show up in a blue font.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the US if it’s not sold
item.description: The law, enacted in April, set a Jan. 19 deadline for TikTok to be sold or else face a ban in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 4 key concepts for Rust beginners
item.description: Few languages are as highly esteemed by developers as Rust, which promises robust memory safety without compromising speed. Rust also has a steep learning curve, though, and some of its programming concepts can feel like a barrier to knowing it well.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications
item.description: New realities of emergency work for emergency responders has necessitated increased use of data transmission of live incident video and file sharing. The post DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Steps to protect yourself while shopping on your phone this holiday season
item.description: The holiday season sees a rise in mobile shopping scams. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you learn how to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google, Microsoft could turn gatekeepers for advanced AI chips
item.description: The US government could designate cloud providers including Google and Microsoft as global gatekeepers for advanced AI chips, a step widely viewed as the latest effort to block China’s access to this critical technology, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World
